title: Arch News: iptables nft backend default + kea manual intervention
content:
## 1) iptables now defaults to nft backend

`iptables-nft` is replaced by `iptables`. Legacy backend is available as `iptables-legacy`.

Important steps:
- If you switch between `iptables-nft`, `iptables`, and `iptables-legacy`, check and restore rule backups if needed:
  - `/etc/iptables/iptables.rules.pacsave`
  - `/etc/iptables/ip6tables.rules.pacsave`
- Test your firewall rules after upgrade.
- If you rely on legacy-only behavior or uncommon xtables extensions, use `iptables-legacy`.

## 2) kea >= 1:3.0.3-6 requires manual intervention

`kea` services now run as dedicated `kea` user instead of `root`.

Important steps (for systems using kea):
- Fix ownership after upgrade:
  - `chown kea: /var/lib/kea/* /var/log/kea/* /run/lock/kea/logger_lockfile`
- Restart kea services:
  - `systemctl try-restart kea-ctrl-agent.service kea-dhcp{4,6,-ddns}.service`
- Add required users to the `kea` group if they need access to kea files/logs/config.
